content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

"Every line of code I write today started with one simple decision—to begin. 💻🚀"I still remember the day I decided to learn programming.
I had no idea where to start.
There were hundreds of programming languages, thousands of tutorials, and millions of people who seemed way ahead of me.
It honestly felt overwhelming.
So, like most beginners, I opened YouTube.
One video became two.
Two became ten.
And before I knew it, I was writing my first few lines of code.
At first, nothing made sense.
Variables, loops, functions...
They all looked confusing.
I made countless mistakes.
Sometimes I spent hours trying to fix an error that was caused by a single missing semicolon.
It was frustrating.
There were moments when I questioned myself.
"Am I really made for this?"
But every time I solved a problem, even a small one, it gave me confidence to continue.
Slowly, coding stopped feeling scary.
I started building small projects.
I learned HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Node.js, and Java.
Every project taught me something new.
Not just about programming...
But about patience, consistency, and learning from failure.
My journey is still at the beginning.
I still search on Google.
I still watch tutorials.
I still get stuck on bugs.
And honestly...
I think that's completely normal.
Technology changes every day.
Nobody knows everything.
The best developers are simply the ones who never stop learning.
When I look back, I realize I've come much farther than I thought.
Not because I'm the smartest person.
But because I kept showing up, even on the days when I felt like giving up.
This is only the beginning of my tech journey.
There's still so much to learn.
And for the first time...
That excites me more than it scares me. 🚀
